question: Which two mechanisms in MPLS provide 'traffic protection' by recovering and reconverging faster than the IGP?
options: Ordered control mode and TTL
Fast re-route and secondary LSPs
LIB and LFIB
Link LDP and Targeted-LDP

question: Match the following loop protection methods in MPLS to their specific purpose.
options: Intended to prevent loops from being created in the first place
Intended to control any loops that get created by accident

correct_answers.A: Intended to prevent loops from being created in the first place
correct_answers.B: Intended to control any loops that get created by accident

question: Match the following MPLS router types to the primary label operation they perform.
options: Push
Swap
Pop

question: Which data table is exclusively used by a 'P' (LSR) router for forwarding MPLS packets?
options: RIB
Route Table
LIB
LFIB

question: Match the 'Special Use' MPLS labels to their specific values.
What is the numeric range for labels that are dynamically assigned by MPLS protocols?
In an Ethernet II frame, where are the MPLS headers placed?
options: Before the destination MAC address
Between the IP header and the TCP/UDP payload
Immediately after the frame header (after dst+src MAC and ethertype)
At the very end of the frame after the payload
correct_answers: Immediately after the frame header (after dst+src MAC and ethertype)

question: What is the size of the 'Label' field in a standard MPLS header?
options: 3 bits
8 bits
20 bits
32 bits

question: How effective is LDP in achieving a 'BGP-free' core?
options: Not effective because it doesn't support iBGP
It provides little benefit as it always follows the IGP
It is excellent because it provides the full-mesh connectivity needed for iBGP
It only works if ECMP is disabled
correct_answers: It is excellent because it provides the full-mesh connectivity needed for iBGP

question: Which of the following are valid names for the label (Marked with an asterisk) used at the very top of an MPLS label stack?
options: Service Label
Inner Label
Transport Label
Bottom Label

question: Which of the following are optimizations used for RSVP session refresh?
options: Hello Protocol
Refresh randomization
Msg ID + ACK
Summary Refresh
All of the above

question: What is the maximum number of LSP-paths that may be associated with a single RSVP-based LSP that is up/up?
In an MPLS L3 VPN, what happens to the customer's L2 frame when it reaches the iLER?
options: It is preserved intact throughout the tunnel
The L2 header is stripped
A new L2 header is added and encapsul   ated inside the service label
The L2 frame is converted to ATM

question: What is a primary difference between L3 VPN and VPLS frame handling?
options: L3 VPN preserves customer L3 TTL; VPLS decrements it
L3 VPN strips customer L2; VPLS preserves customer L2
L3 VPN adds a service label; VPLS does not
VPLS is not compatible with transport tunnels
correct_answers: L3 VPN strips customer L2; VPLS preserves customer L2

question: Match the MPLS tunneling modes to their effect on the customer's IP TTL.
options: Customer TTL gets decremented at every hop
Customer TTL is decremented only at iLER and eLER, effectively hiding the LSRs

correct_answers.A: Customer TTL gets decremented at every hop
correct_answers.B: Customer TTL is decremented only at iLER and eLER, effectively hiding the LSRs

question: Which statement correctly describes a difference between Link LDP and Targeted-LDP (T-LDP)?
options: Link LDP uses unicast; T-LDP uses multicast
Link LDP is for service labels; T-LDP is for transport tunnels
Link LDP requires directly connected neighbors; T-LDP has no such restriction
Link LDP neighbors must be explicitly configured; T-LDP is discovery-based
correct_answers: Link LDP requires directly connected neighbors; T-LDP has no such restriction

question: Match the LDP message type to the connection it maintains.
options: Sent over UDP to maintain the adjacency
Sent over TCP to maintain the session

correct_answers.A: Sent over UDP to maintain the adjacency
correct_answers.B: Sent over TCP to maintain the session

question: What happens if neighboring LDP routers have mismatched configured Hello timeout values?
options: The adjacency fails to form
Both neighbors use the lowest configured value
The session resets every 60 seconds
The routers default to a 3-second timeout
correct_answers: Both neighbors use the lowest configured value

question: In per-platform label space, why might multiple prefix bindings show identical label values for the same FEC?
options: It is purely coincidental
Any/all interfaces receive the same label for a given FEC on that platform
The router has run out of dynamic labels
ECMP is active
correct_answers: Any/all interfaces receive the same label for a given FEC on that platform

question: What is Penultimate Hop Popping (PHP) and why is it used?
options: It is a loop prevention mechanism for high-speed links
It is the popping of labels just before the tunnel destination to reduce resource load on 'small' LER devices
It is a method of encryption for private IP addresses
It is a BGP optimization for urban cell tower sites
correct_answers: It is the popping of labels just before the tunnel destination to reduce resource load on 'small' LER devices
